sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 12:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 12:1
items.html: Malchut is called a “a mirror that has nothing of its own,” or “a mirror that does not shine.” Zeir Anpin is called “a shining mirror.” It is called Light because Zeir Anpin and Malchut are called “the two lights,” since all the illuminations of the lower ones come only from them and not from above them. (Part 3, Chapter 7, item 2)
